Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

Appearances: Michael D. Levinson of Brayton Purcell, LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and opposing party; Richard Lee of Selman Breitman (415.979.2006) for intervenor and moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on September 21, 2012 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on December 8, 2015 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) and pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance is denied. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Michele Lucas, CSR 4017 (Aiken Welch, 510.451.1580) (503/GLW)
